info.name: Starter
info.description:
API collection for the Inertia Starter project.

## Authentication
- All API requests require `X-API-TOKEN` header
- All requests require `Accept-Language` header (`en` or `ar`)
- All API requests require `X-Device-Id` (UUID, required) and `X-Platform` (`web`, `ios`, or `android`) headers. When platform is `ios` or `android`, `X-FCM-Token` is also required (web may omit it). `fcm_token` is no longer accepted in login/register/firebase-login bodies — it always comes via header. These are set explicitly on every request in this collection. Bearer-bearing requests use them for device tracking + fcm refresh; Bearer-less requests use them to lazily upsert a guest user (`is_guest=true`) when `APP_GUESTS=true`, plus a matching `user_devices` row keyed by `device_id`.
- Protected routes require Bearer token from login
- `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` allows only `email` and/or `phone` (e.g. `email`, `phone`, `email,phone`). Username is never a primary identifier.
- Login and registration use a single `identifier` field for the email/phone value. Single identifier configured → validated against that field. Multiple → kind detected from format (email / phone) and validated against the matching field.
- `HAS_USERNAME_FIELD=true` → `username` is **required** at register (format: starts with letter, letters/digits/`_`/`-` after, min 3, not email/phone shape), usable as a login alias (login `identifier` is also searched against the username column), and editable via `update-profile`. Username uniqueness is scoped to api-guard users.
- `HAS_EMAIL_FIELD` / `HAS_PHONE_FIELD` toggle email/phone as optional profile fields when not configured as identifiers.
- Email values are normalized to lowercase on every write and lookup (cross-DB safe).
- `request-identifier-change` (auth) is rate-limited via `throttle:otp` (3/5min) like `forgot-password` and `send-otp`.

## Field-Keyed Errors (Frontend Convention)
Any error tied to a specific request input field is returned as HTTP 422 with the error keyed under the same name as the input parameter. Frontend can look up `errors.{paramName}` to show inline messages next to the matching input.

| Endpoint | Trigger | Error Key |
|----------|---------|-----------|
| `login` | invalid credentials | `password` |
| `login` / `forgot-password` / `verify-forgot-password-otp` / `change-forgot-password` / `register` | identifier validation / user not found | `identifier` |
| `verify-otp` / `verify-forgot-password-otp` / `change-forgot-password` / `verify-email-change` | invalid OTP | `otp` |
| `change-password` | wrong current password | `old_password` |
| `firebase-login` / `link-social-account` | invalid token, provider issues, email mismatch, etc. | `token` |
| `unlink-social-account` | provider not linked, cannot unlink last | `provider` |

State/config errors (account inactive, unauthorized access, feature disabled) remain plain top-level error messages with `errors: null`.

## Variables
- `{{base_url}}` — Base URL (e.g. http://localhost:8000)
- `{{x-api-token}}` — API token for server authentication
- `{{token}}` — Bearer token from login response (returned at top-level `token` field AND duplicated inside `data.token` for convenience)

item.name: Pages

request.description:
Returns all active pages with their translated content.

The `name` and `content` fields are returned in the language specified by the `Accept-Language` header.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Returns a single active page by its slug.

The `name` and `content` fields are returned in the language specified by the `Accept-Language` header.

Returns 404 if the page doesn't exist or is not active.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
Not Found

item.description: Endpoints for retrieving page content. Pages contain translatable content (name and HTML content) that adapts to the Accept-Language header.

request.description:
Returns all active languages available in the system. Use this to know which locales are supported for translations and the `Accept-Language` header.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Languages retrieved successfully.",
  "errors": null,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": 1,
      "code": "en",
      "name": "English",
      "native_name": "English",
      "direction": "ltr",
      "is_default": true,
      "image": null
    },
    {
      "id": 2,
      "code": "ar",
      "name": "Arabic",
      "native_name": "العربية",
      "direction": "rtl",
      "is_default": false,
      "image": null
    }
  ]
}

item.name: Translations

request.description:
Returns all translation key-value pairs for the chosen group in the current locale.

**Query:**
- `group` (optional, default `app`) — `app` or `web`. `api` is server-controlled and not exposed here.

Locale is determined by the `Accept-Language` header.

**Response shape:**
```
{
  "group": "app",
  "locale": "en",
  "translations": { "key1": "value1", ... }
}
```

Fetch on boot and on language switch. Merge into the client i18n store.

Values may contain Laravel `:placeholder` tokens (e.g. `:name`, `:count`). Substitute at render time on the client.

Gated by `HAS_TRANSLATIONS=true`. Rate-limited via `throttle:api`.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success (English)
Success (Arabic)

request.description:
Create or update translations. Locale comes from the `Accept-Language` header.

**Body:**
```json
{
  "group": "app",
  "translations": {
    "key1": "value1",
    "key2": "value2"
  }
}
```

- `group` (optional, default `app`): `app` or `web`. `api` is server-controlled and rejected here.
- `translations`: flat object of `key → value` pairs.

**Behavior:**
- New key → created in the chosen group. Header's locale gets the value; other active locales are seeded as `null` (admin fills them later in the CMS).
- Existing key → only the header's locale value is updated. Other locales untouched.
- Re-call with the same key/group under a different `Accept-Language` to populate that locale.
- Values may contain Laravel `:placeholder` tokens (e.g. `:name`). The admin Translations CMS rejects edits that drop a placeholder present in the default-language value, so keep them intact across locales.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
Success (with non-string value warning)
Empty Body

item.description: Endpoints for managing mobile app translations. Translations are stored in the `app` group and returned based on the `Accept-Language` header.

item.name: Auth
Verified

request.description:
Request a change to the user's email or phone identifier. Kind is auto-detected from `new_identifier` and must match one of the configured identifiers in `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` (allowed: email, phone).

Sends an OTP via the matching channel (email → EmailHelper; phone → SMS).

Username is not an identifier — change it via `Update Profile` instead.

**Errors:**
- 422 `errors.new_identifier` — invalid format, wrong kind, or fails uniqueness/format rules.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Verify the identifier-change OTP and update the user's email or phone column. Username is not handled here (use `Update Profile` instead).

Invalid OTP returns `errors.otp` (422).

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Update the authenticated user's profile info.

**Available fields:** `name`, plus any non-identifier fields that are enabled (e.g. if identifier is `username`, you can update `email` and `phone` if those fields are enabled).

The identifier field itself cannot be changed through this endpoint.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Change the authenticated user's password.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Marks the authenticated user as pending deletion (sets `users.account_deleted_at = now()`) and revokes all tokens. The row stays in DB for the retention window (default 30 days, configurable via `ACCOUNT_DELETION_RETENTION_DAYS` or DevSettings). Logging in with valid credentials before retention expires restores the account (`account_restored: true` in the login response). After retention the row is permanently force-deleted by the global `PurgeDeletedUsersAfterResponse` middleware on the next inbound HTTP request.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Get all social accounts linked to the authenticated user.

Returns an array of social accounts with provider info and whether more can be linked based on configuration.

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
No Social Accounts

request.description:
Link a social account (Google, Apple, etc.) to the authenticated user's account.

**Required:** `token` — Firebase ID token from the social provider.

**Constraints:**
- Social account email must match the user's account email
- Social account must not already be linked to another user
- Provider must be in the allowed providers list (configurable via DevSettings)
- User cannot exceed max social accounts limit (if set)
- Same provider cannot be linked twice to the same user
- Requires email to be configured as a login identifier

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
Email Mismatch
Provider Not Allowed
Provider Already Linked
Max Accounts Reached
Already Linked to Another User

request.description:
Unlink a specific social account from the authenticated user.

**Required:** `provider` — The provider to unlink (e.g. `google.com`, `apple.com`)

**Constraints:**
- User must have a password set OR have other social accounts linked (cannot remove last login method)
- The specified provider must be linked to the user

Requires: Bearer token + verified account.

response.name: Success
Provider Not Linked
Cannot Unlink (No Password)

item.description: Endpoints that require the user's email to be verified.

request.description:
Get the currently authenticated user's profile.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "errors": null,
  "data": {
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Test User",
    "email": "user@example.com",
    "phone": null,
    "verified_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z",
    "is_active": true,
    "created_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z",
    "updated_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z"
  }
}

request.description:
Send an OTP to the authenticated user's email for account verification.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent to the user's email to complete account verification.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Logout the authenticated user and revoke the current access token.

Requires: Bearer token.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Returns the authenticated user's active devices — one row per Sanctum token. Each row carries `device_name`, `platform`, `ip`, `user_agent`, `last_seen_at`, and an `is_current` boolean so the client can highlight (or block revoke on) the device making the request.

Use `auth_config.multi_session` to decide whether to render the Active Devices screen at all. Requires: Bearer token + verified.

response.name: Success

request.description:
Revokes a specific device. Drops its underlying Sanctum token (FK cascade removes the `user_devices` row) and broadcasts `device.revoked` on `private-user.{userId}` with `{ token_id }` so the kicked client clears its local creds.

404 if the device id doesn't belong to the authenticated user.

Requires: Bearer token + verified.

response.name: Success
Not Found

request.description:
Register a new app user.

**Required fields:** `policy_agreed`, `name`, `identifier`, `password`, `password_confirmation`.

**Optional:** `fcm_token`, and any non-identifier fields that are enabled via `HAS_*_FIELD` env (e.g. `phone` when phone is not an identifier).

## Identifier Resolution

The `identifier` field is resolved against `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` (allowed values: `email`, `phone`):
- **Single identifier configured** → validated against that field's rules.
- **`email,phone`** → kind detected from format (email / phone) and validated against the matching field.

## Username

When `HAS_USERNAME_FIELD=true`, `username` is **required** at register. Username is never an identifier — it is a separate column searchable at login as an alias. Format: must start with a letter, then letters/digits/`_`/`-`. Min length 3. Email/phone-shaped values rejected. Uniqueness scoped to api-guard users.

Email values are normalized to lowercase before storage.

Validation errors on the identifier are returned under `errors.identifier`. Other field errors keep their own keys.

**Does not issue a Bearer token.** Account is created and an OTP is sent for verification. The user must call `Login` afterwards to obtain a token (login on an unverified account triggers another verify OTP and returns a token to use with `verify-otp`).

response.name: Success
Success (Username — Auto-Verified)
Validation Failed (Identifier Remap)

request.description:
Login an existing app user.

**Required fields:** `identifier`, `password`

**Optional:**
- `fcm_token` — mobile only. Web admin must omit.
- `remember_me` — 30-day token instead of 1-day.
- `device_name` — free-form label shown in the Active Devices UI (e.g. `iPhone 15 Pro`, `Mohammad's MacBook`).
- `platform` — `ios` | `android` | `web`. Server falls back to User-Agent parsing if omitted.

The `identifier` field is searched across all configured identifier columns (set via `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` env, e.g. `email,phone`). Users can log in with any of their configured identifiers.

Returns a Bearer token plus `data.token_id` — the client should persist this id alongside the token so it can identify itself when receiving the `device.revoked` Pusher event on `private-user.{userId}`.

In single-session mode (`MULTI_SESSION_ENABLED=false`), the new login revokes the user's prior tokens and broadcasts `device.revoked` for each one. In multi-session mode, all tokens stack.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Login successful.",
  "errors": null,
  "token": "2|xyz456tokenhere",
  "data": {
    "user": {
      "id": 1,
      "name": "Test User",
      "email": "user@example.com",
      "phone": null,
      "is_active": true,
      "verified_at": "2026-04-01T12:00:00.000000Z",
      "has_password": true
    },
    "is_verified": true,
    "account_restored": false,
    "token_id": 17,
    "token": "2|xyz456tokenhere"
  }
}

response.name: Invalid Credentials
User Not Found

request.description:
Login or register using Firebase social authentication (Google, Apple, etc.).

**Required:** `token` — Firebase ID token from the social provider.

**Optional:** `fcm_token`

**Prerequisites:**
- Email must be configured as a login identifier (`AUTH_IDENTIFIERS` must include `email`)
- Firebase project must be configured with credentials in `config/firebase.php`

**Behavior:**
- If social account is already linked → login
- If email exists with password → returns error (must use password login)
- If email exists without password (social-only) → links and logs in
- If email doesn't exist → creates new account

Returns a Bearer token on success. User is automatically verified.

response.name: Success (New User)
Success (Existing User)
Provider Not Allowed
Account Exists (Use Password)
Invalid Token
Email Required
Social Auth Not Available

request.description:
Returns the live auth configuration so mobile/web clients can adapt their UI on boot. No body, no Bearer token required.

**Response fields:**
- `identifiers` — array of `email` and/or `phone` (configured via `AUTH_IDENTIFIERS`).
- `has_username_field` — when true, register requires `username` and login can match against username.
- `has_email_field` / `has_phone_field` — when true (and that field is NOT an identifier), it is exposed as an optional profile field.
- `social_providers` — array of allowed Firebase providers (e.g. `["google.com", "apple.com"]`). Empty = all allowed.
- `max_social_accounts` — max linked social accounts per user (0 = unlimited).
- `social_auth_available` — true when email is an identifier (Firebase social auth requires email).
- `is_otp_whatsapp` — phone OTPs go via WhatsApp instead of SMS when true.
- `multi_session` — when true, multiple devices may stay logged in simultaneously and the client should expose the Active Devices screen (`GET /api/devices`). When false, every login revokes the user's previous tokens.

Fetch on app boot, cache in client state.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "errors": null,
  "data": {
    "identifiers": ["email", "phone"],
    "has_username_field": true,
    "has_email_field": false,
    "has_phone_field": false,
    "social_providers": ["google.com", "apple.com"],
    "max_social_accounts": 0,
    "social_auth_available": true,
    "is_otp_whatsapp": false,
    "multi_session": true,
    "app_users": true,
    "app_guests": true
  }
}

request.description:
Detects kind (email / phone / username) from the value and queries the matching column when configured (identifier OR `HAS_*_FIELD` extra). Scoped to api-guard users; queries `withTrashed()` so admin-suspended rows are detectable.

Response:
```
{
  "exists": true|false,
  "pending_deletion": true|false,
  "suspended": true|false,
  "available_channels": ["email", "phone"],
  "has_password": true|false,
  "social_providers": ["google.com", "apple.com"],
  "verified": true|false,
  "is_guest": true|false
}
```

- `pending_deletion: true` — the user requested account deletion. Logging in with valid credentials restores the account.
- `suspended: true` — admin soft-deleted the user. Login returns 403 `api.account_suspended`. Only admin restore (or admin force-delete) frees the identifier; re-register is rejected by the unique rule.
- `available_channels` — OTP delivery channels populated on the user record. Use to pick a `type` for `forgot-password` and for pre-submit uniqueness checks.
- `has_password: false` — social-only account. Frontend should hide the password field and surface social-login buttons instead.
- `social_providers` — Firebase providers already linked (e.g. `google.com`, `apple.com`). Frontend can highlight the matching social button.
- `verified` — `verified_at` is set. Frontend can skip the verification flow if true.
- `is_guest` — the matched row is a guest user. Rare (guests have null email/phone/username) but exposed for completeness.

No authentication required.

response.name: Exists
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "errors": null,
  "data": {
    "exists": true,
    "pending_deletion": false,
    "suspended": false,
    "available_channels": ["email", "phone"],
    "has_password": true,
    "social_providers": ["google.com"],
    "verified": true,
    "is_guest": false
  }
}

response.name: Not Found
response.body:
{
  "success": true,
  "message": "Operation successful",
  "errors": null,
  "data": {
    "exists": false,
    "pending_deletion": false,
    "suspended": false,
    "available_channels": [],
    "has_password": false,
    "social_providers": [],
    "verified": false,
    "is_guest": false
  }
}

request.description:
Request a password reset OTP.

**Body:**
- `identifier` — email, phone, or username.
- `type` (optional) — `"email"` or `"phone"`. When provided, must be one of the user's populated channels (call `Check Identifier` first to discover `available_channels`).

**Channel selection:**
- `type` omitted → priority `email > phone` from the user's populated columns.
- `type` provided → use that channel (or 422 if not populated).

Response includes the actual `channel` used. Phone delivery is SMS by default (or WhatsApp when `IS_OTP_WHATSAPP=true`).

**Errors:**
- 422 `errors.identifier` — user not found OR no deliverable channel populated.
- 422 `errors.type` — requested `type` not populated for this user.

Rate-limited via `throttle:otp` (3/5min).

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
User Not Found

request.description:
Verify the OTP sent for password reset. Must be called before `Change Forgot Password`.

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
User Not Found

request.description:
Set a new password after verifying the forgot password OTP.

**Required:** `email`, `otp` (verified), `password`, `password_confirmation`

No authentication required.

response.name: Success
User Not Found

item.description: User authentication endpoints including registration, login, password reset, OTP verification, and account management.
